items.name: Gate Health Upgrades
items.description: If the gate falls, the run ends. Health upgrades are the cleanest way to stop early collapses while you are still building damage around them.

items.name: Steady Turret Damage
items.description: Consistent turret damage reduces pressure every wave instead of gambling on last-second saves once 67 gets close to the base.

items.name: Turret Coverage
items.description: Good coverage matters because dead angles waste upgrades. A smaller number of well-placed turrets often beats sloppy expansion.

items.name: Economy Upgrades
items.description: Extra income matters once your base is no longer fragile. Economy upgrades shine when they accelerate a defense plan that is already stable.

items.name: Panic Spending
items.description: Throwing currency at random low-impact upgrades during a breach feels active, but it usually means the earlier plan was too thin.

items.name: Greedy Glass Cannon
items.status: Only after you know the waves
items.description: Pure damage with too little gate safety can work, but it is usually worse than a balanced build until you know exactly when 67 spikes your base.
